relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Macedonia becomes an example of grace in the assemblies.
Pressure, joy, and poverty overflow into generosity.
They give according to power and beyond power, freely.
They beg for participation in service to the holy ones.
They give themselves first to the Lord.
Titus is asked to complete this grace among Corinth.
A community rich in gifts is invited to abound in giving-grace too.
This is not command but a test of genuine love.
Messiah’s grace is richness becoming poverty so others become rich.
The earlier will now seeks completion in action.
Eager willing must become completed doing from what is present.
Acceptable giving is according to what one has.
The goal is not burdening one to relieve another.
Abundance meets lack so equality can arise.
Manna wisdom returns: no excess and no lack.
The Divine places eagerness for them in Titus’ heart.
Titus goes freely and eagerly.
A praised sibling accompanies the gift.
The assemblies appoint companions for honorable grace-service.
Care is taken to avoid blame around abundance.
Beautiful things are prepared before Lord and humans.
Another tested eager sibling is sent.
Titus and the siblings are coworkers and assembly-messengers, glory of Messiah.
Love is proven before the assemblies.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 8 turns money into grace-flow. Generosity is not pressure; it is equality, participation, and abundance meeting lack so no one is abandoned.
